Available for opportunities

Sarvysh D Hulani

Explorer Builder BITS Pilani

I'm an engineering student at BITS Pilani with a deep curiosity about the world and how it works. I'm fascinated by finance, technology, business, creativity, and the ideas that shape our lives, but what really excites me is learning how seemingly unrelated things connect. This is my digital garden and field journal: a living record of what I'm learning, building, creating, and discovering along the way. It's a place to think out loud, share ideas, document experiments, and make sense of the world one thought at a time.

I'm designing my own education to understand the world more deeply—and documenting the process of becoming through curiosity, not certainty

View My WorkAbout MeRead My Writing

Studying at

AI Engineering

02

Projects

Featured Projects

Things I am building right now. Each project is a step toward becoming the engineer I want to be.

In Progress

AI Study Companion

An AI-powered study companion built for BITS Pilani students. Uses RAG with fine-tuned embeddings to answer course-specific questions.

Python LangChain FastAPI PostgreSQL
#AI#Education#RAG
Read more
Shipped

Stock Sentiment Analyzer

A real-time sentiment analysis tool that tracks financial news and social media to predict short-term stock movements.

Python Transformers Streamlit Pandas
#Finance#NLP#Data
Read more
Shipped

Campus Connect

A platform connecting BITS Pilani students for skill-sharing, project collaboration, and mentorship.

Next.js Supabase TailwindCSS
#Product#Community#Web

03

Journey

Journey Timeline

Every milestone, every failure, every lesson. This is the path so far.

Milestone Jul 2026

Started Building Digital HQ : My Own Corner Of The Internet

Launched this website — my digital headquarters. The beginning of a documentation journey.

"I wanted a place that was not a portfolio, not a blog, but a living, evolving record of my journey. This is it."
Milestone Jun 2025

Got into BITS Pilani

The day I got my BITS Pilani acceptance. The beginning of everything.

"I remember staring at the screen, refreshing the page, and then seeing the word "Congratulations." Everything changed in that moment."
Milestone Oct 2024

Attended My First Tech Conference

Attended a major AI conference and realized how much there is to learn — and how much I already know.

"Walking through the conference hall, I felt both small and capable at the same time. There is so much to learn, but I am further along than I thought."
Failure May 2024

First Major Failure

A startup idea that completely failed. I spent two months building something nobody wanted.

"I was so focused on building that I forgot to validate. Two months of work, zero users. But I learned more from this failure than from any success."

Lesson Learned

Validate before you build. Talk to users before you write code. The cheapest way to learn is from someone else mistake — the second cheapest is from your own.

View Full Journey

Knowledge

Knowledge Hub

A searchable collection of everything I learn. Notes, research, and insights organized by topic.

Engineering
Intermediate

System Design Basics for AI Engineers

Essential system design concepts every AI engineer should know.

#System Design#Architecture#Scalability
AI
Intermediate

Understanding Transformers: A Deep Dive

A comprehensive guide to understanding the Transformer architecture that powers modern AI.

#Deep Learning#NLP#Transformers
Finance
Beginner

Introduction to Venture Capital

Understanding how VC works — from term sheets to valuations to exit strategies.

#VC#Investing#Startups
Consulting

Consulting Frameworks for Problem Solving

Mental models from top consulting firms that help structure any problem.

#Problem Solving#Frameworks#Strategy
Productivity
Beginner

Building Productive Habits That Last

A practical framework for building habits that stick, based on behavioral science.

#Habits#Productivity#Self-Improvement

05

Resources

Resource Library

Curated learning resources that have shaped my thinking. Each one comes with personal notes.

AI Advanced
Essential

Attention Is All You Need

The paper that introduced the Transformer architecture. A must-read for anyone in AI.

Read this multiple times. Each time I understand something new. The key insight is that attention replaces recurrence entirely.

Visit Resource
AI Intermediate
Highly Recommended

Designing Machine Learning Systems

Chip Huyen comprehensive guide to ML systems design. Covers everything from data pipelines to deployment.

The best book on practical ML I have read. The chapters on feature stores and model monitoring are gold.

Visit Resource
Career Beginner
Essential

The Almanack of Naval Ravikant

Wisdom on wealth, happiness, and building from one of the most original thinkers in tech.

Read this when I feel lost. Naval thinking on leverage and specific knowledge changed how I approach my career.

Visit Resource
Startups Beginner
Highly Recommended

Zero to One

Peter Thiel book on building the future. Essential reading for entrepreneurs.

The concept of going from 0 to 1 vs 1 to n changed how I think about innovation. Monopoly thinking is underrated.

Visit Resource
Browse All Resources

07

Reading

Reading

Books that shaped my thinking. Notes, quotes, and ratings included.

Reading

Designing Machine Learning Systems

Chip Huyen

The most practical ML book I have found. Covers the entire ML lifecycle from data to deployment.

"The goal of ML is not to build models but to build systems that create value."

Finished

Zero to One

Peter Thiel

A short but dense book. The core idea: true innovation means creating something entirely new, not iterating on what exists.

"Competition is for losers. Monopolies are good for everyone."

Finished

Thinking, Fast and Slow

Daniel Kahneman

A profound book about how we think and make decisions. System 1 vs System 2 thinking explains so much about human behavior.

"We can be blind to the obvious, and we are also blind to our blindness."

Finished

The Pragmatic Programmer

Andrew Hunt, David Thomas

Timeless advice for software developers. The tips on DRY, orthogonality, and tracer bullets are worth the price alone.

"A pragmatic programmer thinks beyond the immediate problem, placing it in the context of the larger system."

View All Books
08

09

About

The beginning of a story

I was the kid who took things apart before I understood how they went together. Radios, old motors, whatever I could get my hands on — and then too many books, always reaching a little past what I could actually digest. Most people grow out of that phase. I never did. I just got better tools. I'm an engineering student at BITS Pilani, and for a long time I thought that meant I was supposed to pick a lane. Science pulled at me. Then investment banking. Then AI. Then this restless urge to stop reading about how other people build things and just build them myself. I kept waiting for the moment I'd narrow down to one thing. That moment never came. Eventually, I stopped waiting for it. What I noticed instead is that none of these things were ever really separate. Valuing a company and designing a system run on the same instinct — figure out what's actually true underneath the noise. A pitch deck and a well-written essay are both just attempts to make an idea land clearly in someone else's head. Once I saw that, specializing early started to feel like the wrong goal. Connecting felt like the right one. So I started building the infrastructure for that — literally. This website isn't a resume. It's closer to a living archive: my projects, my writing, what I'm learning right now, and where all of it is headed. Nothing here is polished for an interviewer. It's just current. Alongside it, there's In Search Of — my newsletter, where I write in public about whatever I'm actually wrestling with the moment I sit down to write it. No pretending to have it figured out. Just the thinking, in real time. None of this is a side project running alongside my "real" path. This is the path. Proof, collected in public, that I'd rather understand something deeply and build with it than collect credentials and call that understanding. I think the next decade belongs to people who can bridge the gap between ambitious ideas and real, working systems — the ones who can move from a whiteboard to a working prototype without losing the idea in translation. That's the person I'm trying to become, one project, one essay, one late night at a time

— Sarvysh D Hulani

Interests

Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning Product Design & UX Investing & Financial Markets Philosophy & Psychology Cricket & Chess Science Fiction & Worldbuilding
Read My Full Story

Curiosity Over Comfort

I choose the harder path when it teaches me more.

Build in Public

Ideas are cheap. Shipping is everything. I document as I go.

Depth Over Breadth

I go deep on the things that matter rather than skimming everything.

Genuine Always

No personas, no pretending. What you see is what you get.
